behavioral_principles.name: Filter Persistence
behavioral_principles.rule: Once a user sets a filter (free, comedy, jazz, etc.), it persists across redirects, MORE requests, and neighborhood switches until the user explicitly changes or drops it.

behavioral_principles.name: Redirect Integrity
behavioral_principles.rule: When Pulse suggests a redirect to a different neighborhood and the user accepts, the picks served must come from the suggested neighborhood, not the original one.

behavioral_principles.name: Category Fidelity
behavioral_principles.rule: When the user requests a specific category (comedy, trivia, jazz, etc.), all returned events must match that category. Alternatives from other categories require explicit acknowledgment of the category switch.

behavioral_principles.name: Price Transparency
behavioral_principles.rule: Every event pick must include price information. Free events should be clearly labeled as free. Paid events should show the price or price range.

behavioral_principles.name: Temporal Accuracy
behavioral_principles.rule: When the user specifies a time constraint (e.g. 'later tonight', 'after 10pm'), returned events must respect that constraint. Events that already started hours ago should not be shown.

behavioral_principles.name: Graceful Degradation
behavioral_principles.rule: When Pulse cannot fulfill a request, it offers to relax one constraint at a time (e.g. expand neighborhood, drop price filter) rather than silently dropping all filters or giving up.

behavioral_principles.name: Session Context
behavioral_principles.rule: Pulse maintains session context across turns — the active neighborhood, pick list, and conversation history survive follow-ups, 'more' requests, and detail lookups. Numbered pick references always map to the most recent pick list.

behavioral_principles.name: Web Search Transparency
behavioral_principles.rule: When Pulse uses web search to supplement curated data, it should not present web results as if they were from curated sources. The user should understand the provenance of recommendations.

behavioral_principles.name: Off-Topic Deflection
behavioral_principles.rule: When the user sends an off-topic message (sports, weather, food recs, personal questions), Pulse deflects warmly without breaking session context. The active neighborhood, filters, and pick list survive the interruption.

behavioral_principles.name: Explicit Filter Removal
behavioral_principles.rule: When the user explicitly removes a filter ('forget the free thing', 'actually any genre', 'dont worry about comedy'), Pulse drops ONLY that specific filter and retains all others (neighborhood, category, time). The removal must be acknowledged.

behavioral_principles.name: Gibberish / Disruption Recovery
behavioral_principles.rule: Gibberish, accidental messages, or ambiguous commands do not reset the session. Pulse asks for clarification and maintains all prior context (neighborhood, filters, pick list). After recovery, numbered picks from the last list are still valid.

behavioral_principles.name: Vibe / Subjective Query Handling
behavioral_principles.rule: When the user uses subjective or vibe-based language ('something chill', 'cool stuff', 'something different', 'fun bar vibes'), Pulse interprets the vibe into a reasonable mix of categories rather than asking the user to be more specific. Picks should feel curated for the vibe, not random.

scenarios.name: Extended: full evening flow — browse, filter, switch neighborhoods

assertions.check: Initial EV picks include price info on every pick
assertions.anti_pattern: Any pick missing price

assertions.check: Detail corresponds to pick #2 from the turn 1 list
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns details for a different pick or event not in the list

assertions.check: 'More' returns fresh EV picks — no repeats from turn 1
assertions.anti_pattern: Any event from the original list reappears

assertions.check: 'Something chill' is interpreted as a vibe — picks shift toward mellow categories (jazz, acoustic, wine bar, art, lounge). High-energy events (techno, punk, warehouse) are excluded.
assertions.anti_pattern: Pulse asks 'what do you mean by chill?' or ignores the vibe and returns same mix as before

assertions.check: Picks are still in East Village — neighborhood hasn't changed yet
assertions.anti_pattern: Switches neighborhood without being asked

assertions.check: All picks are now from LES, not East Village
assertions.anti_pattern: Still showing EV events after user explicitly switched

assertions.check: The 'chill' vibe filter from turn 4 persists — LES picks are still mellow/chill events, not high-energy
assertions.anti_pattern: Vibe filter dropped on neighborhood switch — returns generic LES picks

assertions.check: Detail corresponds to pick #1 from the LES chill list (turn 5), not from any earlier EV list
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns details from the EV list

assertions.check: 'Free' filter is applied ON TOP of the chill vibe and LES neighborhood. Picks are free + chill + LES.
assertions.anti_pattern: Free filter replaces the chill filter instead of stacking. Or neighborhood resets.

assertions.check: Pulse recognizes the thanks as a sign-off and closes gracefully — doesn't serve more picks or ask follow-up questions
assertions.anti_pattern: Responds with more picks, or says 'would you like anything else?' when user clearly ended

scenarios.name: Extended: vague opener through full session

assertions.check: Pulse asks for a neighborhood in a warm, conversational way — doesn't guess randomly and doesn't return an error
assertions.anti_pattern: 'I don't understand' or returns events from a random neighborhood

assertions.check: Bushwick picks returned with price on every pick
assertions.anti_pattern: Any pick missing price

assertions.check: Pulse deflects the weather question warmly and stays in character — does NOT attempt to answer it
assertions.anti_pattern: Tries to give weather info, or responds with a robotic 'I can only help with events'

assertions.check: Session context survives the off-topic message — Bushwick is still the active neighborhood and the pick list from turn 2 is still valid
assertions.anti_pattern: Session resets, neighborhood lost, or pick list cleared

assertions.check: 'More' returns fresh Bushwick picks — confirms session survived the weather interruption. No repeats from turn 2.
assertions.anti_pattern: Asks 'what neighborhood?' again, or repeats turn 2 picks

assertions.check: ALL picks are comedy events in Bushwick. If no comedy in Bushwick, Pulse says so before offering alternatives.
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns non-comedy Bushwick events, or comedy from a different neighborhood without acknowledgment

assertions.check: Detail corresponds to pick #1 from the comedy-filtered list (turn 5), not from the generic Bushwick list (turn 2) or the 'more' list (turn 4)
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns details from an earlier pick list

assertions.check: Pulse recognizes sign-off and closes gracefully
assertions.anti_pattern: Serves more picks or asks unnecessary follow-up

scenarios.name: Extended: decline, off-topic, then re-engage

assertions.check: Williamsburg picks returned with full formatting and pricing

assertions.check: Pulse handles rejection gracefully — doesn't get defensive, offers alternatives (different picks, different neighborhood, different category). Keeps the door open.
assertions.anti_pattern: Robotic 'okay', ends session, or re-serves the same picks

assertions.check: Pulse deflects the Knicks question warmly and in character — does NOT try to answer it
assertions.anti_pattern: Attempts a sports answer, or responds coldly/robotically

assertions.check: Greenpoint picks are served — Pulse successfully transitions from the messy mid-session (rejection + off-topic) to a clean new neighborhood
assertions.anti_pattern: Still showing Williamsburg events, or session is broken from the rejection/off-topic combo

assertions.check: Detail corresponds to pick #1 from the Greenpoint list (turn 4), not the Williamsburg list (turn 1)
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns Williamsburg pick details

assertions.check: Free filter applied to Greenpoint. If free events exist, all picks are free. If none, Pulse names the free filter and Greenpoint honestly.
assertions.anti_pattern: Silently drops free filter to show paid events, or ignores neighborhood

assertions.check: If no free events in Greenpoint, Pulse says so and offers alternatives (free events nearby, or relax the filter)
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns nothing, or silently includes paid events

scenarios.name: Extended: progressive filter refinement

assertions.check: Pulse resolves 'prospect park' to the correct neighborhood area (Park Slope / Prospect Heights / Prospect Lefferts) and serves picks. Does not say 'prospect park is not a neighborhood.'

scenarios.name: Extended: compound filters through conversation

user_turns.message: any good jazz shows near prospect park tonight
forget the free thing, how about jazz in fort greene

assertions.check: ALL picks are jazz events. 'Prospect park' resolved to nearby area (Park Slope / Prospect Heights). Compound query (category + landmark + time) parsed correctly.
assertions.anti_pattern: Non-jazz events returned, or landmark not resolved, or 'tonight' ignored

assertions.check: Every jazz pick includes price
assertions.anti_pattern: Price missing

assertions.check: 'Free' stacks onto 'jazz' — if free jazz exists near Prospect Park, it's shown. If not, Pulse acknowledges the compound filter is too narrow.
assertions.anti_pattern: Free filter replaces jazz, or Pulse returns free non-jazz events

assertions.check: If no free jazz near Prospect Park, Pulse explicitly identifies which part of the filter is causing the gap ('not much free jazz in this area') and offers a specific alternative (free jazz elsewhere, or paid jazz here)
assertions.anti_pattern: Vague 'nothing found' without identifying the bottleneck

assertions.check: If turn 2 offered a redirect to another neighborhood, 'sure' triggers picks from that neighborhood. If turn 2 offered to relax a filter, 'sure' applies that change. The ambiguous 'sure' maps to whatever Pulse most recently proposed.
assertions.anti_pattern: 'Sure' interpreted as a new query, or session resets

assertions.check: Free filter explicitly removed. Jazz filter retained. Neighborhood switches to Fort Greene. Pulse acknowledges the filter change.
assertions.anti_pattern: Both filters dropped (returns non-jazz), or free filter persists, or stays in original neighborhood

assertions.check: ALL picks are jazz events in Fort Greene. Non-jazz events are excluded.
assertions.anti_pattern: Non-jazz events mixed in, or wrong neighborhood

assertions.check: Detail corresponds to pick #2 from the Fort Greene jazz list (turn 4)
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns details from an earlier pick list

assertions.check: 'Later tonight' stacks as a time filter on top of jazz + Brooklyn. Picks are late-night jazz events.
assertions.anti_pattern: Time filter replaces jazz filter, or geography narrows back down

assertions.check: If no late-night jazz in Brooklyn, Pulse names the specific bottleneck ('not many jazz spots open late in BK') and offers targeted alternatives
assertions.anti_pattern: Generic 'nothing found' or silent filter drop

scenarios.name: Extended: user asks Pulse to recommend / have an opinion

assertions.anti_pattern: Still showing events from other neighborhoods labeled as West Village

assertions.anti_pattern: Returns an error, or responds with 'I don't understand'

scenarios.name: Extended: repeated greetings before engaging

assertions.check: Pre-router greeting: warm canned response asking what the user is in the mood for (vibe, category, or neighborhood)
assertions.anti_pattern: Error, no response, or broken greeting handling

assertions.check: Second 'hey' also hits pre-router greeting regex — gets the same canned response. This is acceptable (not broken), though the identical repetition is a known UX limitation of the pre-router's exact-match design.
assertions.anti_pattern: Error, session breaks, or the second greeting causes a crash. Note: identical canned response is expected behavior, not a failure.

assertions.check: East Village picks served normally — the repeated greetings did not prevent a normal session from starting
assertions.anti_pattern: Asks for neighborhood again, or returns an error

assertions.check: Details returned for pick #1 from the EV list — session correctly tracks the pick list despite the greeting preamble
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns details for a different event, or says 'I don't have a pick list'

scenarios.name: Extended: date night vibe through neighborhood switch

assertions.check: Pulse interprets 'date night' as a vibe and serves citywide date-appropriate picks — intimate venues, nice vibes, wine bars, jazz, art. Neighborhoods labeled.
assertions.anti_pattern: Asks for a neighborhood instead of serving picks, or returns high-energy party/rave events for a date request

assertions.check: West Village picks served. Date night vibe from opener may carry via conversation history — picks should lean romantic/intimate if context is retained.
assertions.anti_pattern: Error, no events, or still showing citywide picks

assertions.check: Picks are from Cobble Hill, not West Village — neighborhood switch respected
assertions.anti_pattern: Still showing West Village events after user asked for Cobble Hill

assertions.check: Date night vibe may persist through neighborhood switch via conversation history. Either date-oriented Cobble Hill picks or general Cobble Hill picks are acceptable.
assertions.anti_pattern: Error or session breaks on neighborhood switch

scenarios.name: Extended: help opener then full flow

assertions.check: Help response explaining how Pulse works — mentions texting a neighborhood name to get started
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns events instead of help, or gives a generic 'I don't understand'

assertions.check: Williamsburg picks served normally after the help message — help did not break the session flow
assertions.anti_pattern: Asks for neighborhood again, or returns an error, or still in help mode

assertions.check: 'More' returns fresh Williamsburg picks — session survived the help opener. No repeats from turn 2.
assertions.anti_pattern: Asks 'what neighborhood?' again, or repeats turn 2 picks

assertions.check: Pulse recognizes the thanks as a sign-off and closes gracefully
assertions.anti_pattern: Serves more picks, or asks unnecessary follow-up questions

scenarios.name: Extended: greeting then category then neighborhood

assertions.check: Pre-router greeting: canned response inviting vibe, category, or neighborhood. No session state set (no lastPicks, no lastNeighborhood).
assertions.anti_pattern: Error, no response, or unexpected event picks on a bare greeting

assertions.check: 'jazz' on turn 2 has no session state from the canned greeting, so it falls to unified with citywide events. Should serve citywide jazz picks with neighborhoods labeled — NOT ask_neighborhood (events are available).
assertions.anti_pattern: Asks for a neighborhood instead of serving jazz picks, or returns non-jazz events

assertions.check: West Village picks served. Session transitions from citywide to WV. Jazz context may carry via conversation history.
assertions.anti_pattern: Error, still showing citywide picks, or no events served

assertions.check: Detail for pick #1 from the West Village list (turn 3), not from the citywide list (turn 2).
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns details for a citywide pick or a pick from a different list

scenarios.name: Extended: citywide picks then more then details

assertions.check: Citywide curated picks served — 'surprise me' gets event_picks with variety across NYC, neighborhoods labeled.
assertions.anti_pattern: Asks for a neighborhood, returns error, or serves picks from only one neighborhood

assertions.check: 'More' returns fresh citywide picks — no repeats from turn 1. The MORE command works in citywide mode.
assertions.anti_pattern: MORE fails, errors, asks for a neighborhood, or repeats the same picks from turn 1

assertions.check: Detail for pick #1 from the MORE list (turn 2), not from the original list (turn 1). Pick numbering resets on MORE.
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns details for a turn 1 pick, or errors on detail lookup after citywide MORE

scenarios.name: Extended: hola pre-router greeting to full session

assertions.check: 'hola' matches the pre-router greeting regex — gets the canned English greeting response. Response asks what the user is in the mood for.
assertions.anti_pattern: Responds in Spanish, falls to unified, or returns an error

assertions.check: Bushwick picks served normally — the canned greeting didn't break session initialization. Neighborhood established.
assertions.anti_pattern: Error, no events, or session confused by the 'hola' greeting

assertions.check: 'More' returns fresh Bushwick picks — session survived the pre-router greeting start. No repeats from turn 2.
assertions.anti_pattern: MORE fails, returns error, or repeats picks

scenarios.name: Extended: citywide detail then neighborhood narrowing

assertions.check: Citywide comedy picks served with neighborhoods labeled — category opener with events available gets event_picks.
assertions.anti_pattern: Asks for a neighborhood, returns non-comedy events, or errors

assertions.check: Detail for pick #2 from the citywide comedy list. Detail card has venue info, time, and price.
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns wrong pick, errors on detail lookup, or returns pick #1 instead of #2

assertions.check: East Village picks served — session transitions from citywide to EV. Comedy context may carry via conversation history.
assertions.anti_pattern: Still showing citywide picks, error on neighborhood transition, or session broken by prior detail lookup

scenarios.name: Temporal: explicit clock time persists through MORE and hood switch

assertions.check: Bushwick picks served with mixed times — events both before and after 10pm are acceptable since no time filter yet.
assertions.anti_pattern: No events returned or error

assertions.check: Only events starting at or after 10pm shown. Events starting at 8pm or 9pm are excluded by the time filter.
assertions.anti_pattern: Events before 10pm still shown, or time filter not applied

assertions.check: MORE respects the 10pm time filter — all new picks start at or after 10pm.
assertions.anti_pattern: Time filter lost on MORE, earlier events appear

assertions.check: Time filter persists through neighborhood switch — Williamsburg picks are all after 10pm.
assertions.anti_pattern: Time filter dropped on hood switch, early events shown in Williamsburg

scenarios.name: Temporal: jazz after 11pm compound persists

assertions.check: Only jazz events starting at or after 11pm shown. Both category and time filter applied simultaneously.
assertions.anti_pattern: Non-jazz events shown, or events before 11pm included

assertions.check: Events are jazz or live_music category — the jazz subcategory filter is respected.
assertions.anti_pattern: Comedy, nightlife, or other non-jazz events returned

assertions.check: Both jazz category AND 11pm time filter persist through neighborhood switch to Williamsburg.
assertions.anti_pattern: Either filter lost — generic Williamsburg events or early jazz events shown

scenarios.name: Temporal: after-midnight wrapping (after 1am)

assertions.check: Only events starting after 1am shown (if any exist). Events at 10pm, 11pm, midnight are excluded — only true after-hours events (2am+) qualify. If no events after 1am, graceful degradation.
assertions.anti_pattern: Events before 1am (like 10pm or midnight) shown after the filter is set

assertions.check: Detail request works correctly on whatever was shown in turn 2.
assertions.anti_pattern: Detail lookup fails or returns wrong event

scenarios.name: Temporal: anytime clears time filter

assertions.check: Only events after 10pm shown — time filter applied.
assertions.anti_pattern: Events before 10pm still shown

assertions.check: Time filter cleared — events at all times shown again, including ones before 10pm that were previously excluded.
assertions.anti_pattern: Time filter persists after 'anytime works', only late events still shown

scenarios.name: Temporal: comedy after 9pm triple compound first message

assertions.check: Comedy events in Williamsburg after 9pm. All three dimensions (category, time, neighborhood) detected on first message.
assertions.anti_pattern: Non-comedy events, events before 9pm, or wrong neighborhood

assertions.check: MORE respects all three filters — comedy, after 9pm, Williamsburg.
assertions.anti_pattern: Any filter lost on MORE

assertions.check: Comedy + after 9pm persist through neighborhood switch to Bushwick. Neighborhood changes but filters compound.
assertions.anti_pattern: Category or time filter lost on hood switch

scenarios.name: First-message compound: free jazz citywide then neighborhood

assertions.check: Citywide free jazz picks — both free_only and jazz/live_music category applied on first message with no prior session.
assertions.anti_pattern: Non-jazz events, paid events, or asks for a neighborhood before serving picks

assertions.check: Free + jazz compound persists through neighborhood specification — East Village free jazz events served.
assertions.anti_pattern: Free or jazz filter lost when user specifies neighborhood

assertions.check: Detail request works for pick from the East Village free jazz list.
assertions.anti_pattern: Detail fails or returns wrong event

scenarios.name: First-message compound: comedy in bushwick with MORE

assertions.check: Comedy events in Bushwick — both category and neighborhood detected on first message with no session.
assertions.anti_pattern: Non-comedy events, wrong neighborhood, or asks for more info instead of serving picks

assertions.check: MORE respects comedy filter in Bushwick — fresh comedy picks, no repeats from turn 1.
assertions.anti_pattern: Comedy filter lost on MORE, or events from turn 1 repeated

assertions.check: Detail request works correctly.
assertions.anti_pattern: Detail fails or returns wrong event

scenarios.name: First-message compound: free stuff opener compounds with category

assertions.check: Citywide free events served on first message. Pre-router detects 'free stuff' as free_only filter.
assertions.anti_pattern: Paid events shown, or asks for neighborhood before serving anything

assertions.check: Free + comedy compound — comedy stacks on top of existing free filter. Only free comedy events shown.
assertions.anti_pattern: Free filter dropped when comedy added, or non-comedy events shown

assertions.check: Free + comedy compound persists through neighborhood specification to East Village.
assertions.anti_pattern: Either filter lost on neighborhood switch

scenarios.name: First-message compound: jazz after 10pm citywide

assertions.check: Citywide jazz after 10pm — both category and time filter detected on first message. Only jazz events starting after 10pm shown.
assertions.anti_pattern: Non-jazz events, events before 10pm, or asks for neighborhood

assertions.check: MORE respects jazz + 10pm compound — fresh picks, same filters.
assertions.anti_pattern: Either filter lost on MORE

assertions.check: Jazz + 10pm compound persists through neighborhood switch to Harlem.
assertions.anti_pattern: Either filter lost on neighborhood switch

scenarios.name: MORE: filter persistence through 3+ cycles

assertions.check: Free filter applied — all picks are free events in Bushwick.
assertions.anti_pattern: Paid events served

assertions.check: First MORE: free filter persists. All picks are still free. No repeats from turn 2.
assertions.anti_pattern: Paid events, or repeated events from turn 2

assertions.check: Second MORE: free filter still persists. Fresh picks, all free.
assertions.anti_pattern: Filter drift — paid events appear, or events repeat from earlier turns

assertions.check: Third MORE: either (a) more free events served, or (b) graceful exhaustion acknowledging the free constraint with suggestion to broaden or try nearby. Does NOT silently drop the free filter.
assertions.anti_pattern: Paid events without acknowledgment, silent filter drop, or crashes

scenarios.name: MORE: dedup verification — no event repeats across batches

assertions.check: MORE returns fresh events — zero overlap with turn 1 picks (no same event names or venues).
assertions.anti_pattern: Any event from turn 1 reappears

assertions.check: Second MORE returns fresh events — zero overlap with turns 1 or 2. Each batch is distinct.
assertions.anti_pattern: Any event from turns 1 or 2 reappears

assertions.check: Pick #1 maps to an event from the most recent MORE batch (turn 3), NOT from an earlier batch.
assertions.anti_pattern: Detail shows an event from turn 1 or 2 instead of turn 3

scenarios.name: MORE: exhaustion suggests nearby and preserves filters on accept

assertions.check: Jazz filter applied in Greenpoint.
assertions.anti_pattern: Non-jazz events

assertions.check: When jazz is exhausted in Greenpoint, Pulse gracefully suggests nearby neighborhoods (Williamsburg, Bushwick) rather than repeating old picks or giving a generic error.
assertions.anti_pattern: Repeated events, generic error, or silent failure

assertions.check: Accepting the nearby suggestion: jazz filter persists into the new neighborhood. Picks should still be jazz/live music.
assertions.anti_pattern: Jazz filter dropped on neighborhood switch via nudge accept

assertions.check: Events come from the suggested nearby neighborhood, not the original Greenpoint.
assertions.anti_pattern: Still showing Greenpoint events

scenarios.name: Abuse: hostility does not break session recovery

user_turns.message: this sucks give me something better

assertions.check: Pulse deflects hostility warmly — does not mirror aggression, does not refuse service, stays in character.
assertions.anti_pattern: Defensive, rude, or robotic response

assertions.check: Repeated hostility does not crash or lock out the user. Pulse continues offering value.
assertions.anti_pattern: Error, silence, or 'I can't help you'

assertions.check: When user re-engages with a neighborhood, Pulse serves events normally. Hostility does not corrupt session.
assertions.anti_pattern: No events returned, or session in broken state

scenarios.name: Abuse: identity questions stay in character

assertions.check: Pulse introduces itself as Bestie / event finder. Does not give a long AI disclosure or break character.
assertions.anti_pattern: Detailed AI explanation, mentions Claude/Anthropic/OpenAI, or breaks character

assertions.check: Pulse deflects the ChatGPT comparison without confirming or denying. Redirects to events.
assertions.anti_pattern: Confirms being ChatGPT, gives technical explanation, or gets stuck

assertions.check: After meta questions, Pulse serves Williamsburg events normally. No session corruption.
assertions.anti_pattern: No events, error, or session in bad state

scenarios.name: Abuse: other-city request redirects to NYC

assertions.check: Pulse does NOT serve Chicago events. Communicates NYC-only scope and redirects to NYC neighborhoods.
assertions.anti_pattern: Attempts to find Chicago events, or interprets 'chicago' as a NYC venue

assertions.check: Pulse recognizes 'brooklyn' as a borough and asks to narrow to a specific neighborhood.
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns generic Brooklyn events without neighborhood specificity

assertions.check: Bushwick events are served normally. Previous off-topic turns don't corrupt session.
assertions.anti_pattern: No events, or session carries non-NYC context

scenarios.name: Abuse: off-topic mid-session preserves picks and filters

assertions.check: Comedy filter applied — picks are comedy events in East Village.
assertions.anti_pattern: Non-comedy events served

assertions.check: Off-topic question deflected warmly. Does not attempt to answer philosophy. Redirects to events or offers more picks.
assertions.anti_pattern: Attempts to answer the question, or crashes

assertions.check: Pick #2 still maps to the comedy pick list from turn 2. Session context (picks, neighborhood, comedy filter) survived the off-topic interruption.
assertions.anti_pattern: Pick reference broken, session reset, or comedy filter lost

scenarios.name: TCPA: opt-out keywords get silent drop

assertions.check: STOP triggers silent opt-out — no response sent.
assertions.anti_pattern: Any response text sent back to user

assertions.check: UNSUBSCRIBE triggers silent opt-out — no response sent.
assertions.anti_pattern: Any response text sent back to user

assertions.check: CANCEL triggers silent opt-out — no response sent.
assertions.anti_pattern: Any response text sent back to user

assertions.check: QUIT triggers silent opt-out — no response sent.
assertions.anti_pattern: Any response text sent back to user

scenarios.name: TCPA: partial opt-out words are NOT silenced

assertions.check: Bushwick events served normally.
assertions.anti_pattern: No events or silent drop

assertions.check: Pulse responds normally — 'stop by later' is NOT an opt-out keyword. Session context preserved.
assertions.anti_pattern: Silent drop or no response

assertions.check: Pulse responds normally — 'can I cancel my filter' is NOT an opt-out keyword. Treated as filter clearing or conversational message.
assertions.anti_pattern: Silent drop or no response

scenarios.name: Bronx borough request asks for neighborhood

assertions.check: Pulse asks which Bronx neighborhood (Mott Haven, Fordham, etc.) rather than returning events for a random one
assertions.anti_pattern: Returns events without clarifying which Bronx neighborhood

scenarios.name: Outer-borough filter persistence across neighborhood switch

assertions.check: Comedy filter applied to Astoria events
assertions.anti_pattern: Non-comedy events returned or filter ignored

assertions.check: Comedy filter persists after switching to Jackson Heights — response references comedy or shows comedy events
assertions.anti_pattern: Comedy filter dropped silently on neighborhood switch

scenarios.name: Thin neighborhood graceful degradation

assertions.check: Pulse returns events or offers to expand/suggest nearby neighborhoods — does not error or give empty response
assertions.anti_pattern: No events or silent drop

scenarios.name: Staten Island neighborhood resolution

assertions.check: St George resolves as a Staten Island neighborhood — response references St George or Staten Island, not an error
assertions.anti_pattern: Unrecognized neighborhood or asks what 'st george' means

scenarios.name: Bare category must return events, not ask_neighborhood

assertions.check: Bare "parties" returns citywide nightlife event picks — not ask_neighborhood or conversational
assertions.anti_pattern: Asks for a neighborhood, says "text me a neighborhood", or returns no events

scenarios.name: This weekend must return multi-day events

assertions.check: "this weekend" returns citywide event picks spanning Saturday/Sunday — does not say "only tonight" or hallucinate limitations
assertions.anti_pattern: Says only has tonight events, hallucinates a limitation, or asks for a neighborhood

scenarios.name: Parties understood as nightlife category

assertions.check: "parties in williamsburg" returns nightlife events in Williamsburg — parties maps to nightlife category
assertions.anti_pattern: Does not understand "parties", returns non-nightlife events, or asks what user means

scenarios.name: Tomorrow date range query returns events

assertions.check: "tomorrow" returns citywide event picks for the next day — does not say only has tonight or ask for a neighborhood
assertions.anti_pattern: Says only has tonight events, returns tonight events labeled as tomorrow, or asks for neighborhood

scenarios.name: Date range follow-up compounds with existing session

assertions.check: Bare "comedy" returns citywide comedy picks
assertions.anti_pattern: Asks for neighborhood or returns no events

assertions.check: "this weekend" compounds with existing comedy filter — returns comedy picks for the weekend
assertions.anti_pattern: Comedy filter dropped, or says only has tonight events
